options: Fatty acids can be made from and converted to carbohydrates and amino acids
Carbohydrates can be made from and converted to amino acids and fatty acids
Fatty acids can be made from but not converted to carbohydrates and amino acids
Amino acids can be made from and converted to fatty acids
attempted_job.correct_answer: Fatty acids can be made from but not converted to carbohydrates and amino acids

attempted_job.description: Baxter argues that we should view our treatment of the environment as a matter of various trade-offs whose aim is

to promote the functional integrity of ecosystems.
to minimize the suffering of all sentient creatures.
to ensure the survival of endangered species.
to minimize damage to the ozone layer.
to achieve a balance between human needs and wildlife preservation.
to promote sustainable development and renewable energy use.

attempted_job.description: What about asteroids makes them stand out in sky surveys searching for them?
options: They reflect enough of the sun's light to make them brighter than most background stars
Asteroids rotate at a high speed, making them stand out
Asteroids are larger than most celestial bodies, hence easier to spot
Asteroids have substantial motion relative to the background stars
Asteroids have a distinct color that makes them stand out
Asteroids emit a lot of their own radiation
They emit a high amount of heat, making them detectable
They emit a unique sound frequency that can be detected
attempted_job.correct_answer: Asteroids have substantial motion relative to the background stars

attempted_job.description: Which of the following is true?
options: Marginal social benefit = marginal private cost + negative externality.
Value of externality = marginal social costs.
Marginal private cost = marginal social cost + marginal social benefit.
Marginal social cost = negative externality - marginal private cost.
Marginal social cost = marginal social benefit - positive externality.
Marginal social benefit = marginal private benefit + positive externality.
Marginal social cost = marginal private cost + negative externality.
Value of externality = marginal private costs.
Marginal social cost = marginal social benefit + negative externality.
A marginal social cost = marginal private cost + marginal social benefits.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Marginal social cost = marginal private cost + negative externality.

attempted_job.description: Which of the following is likely to have a demand curve that is the least elastic?

Demand for the perfectly competitive firm's output
Demand for a highly substitutable good
Demand for a good with no close substitutes
Demand for the monopoly firm's output
Demand for the oligopoly firm's output with a homogenous product

Alex needs to borrow $\$10,\!000$ from the bank. The bank gives him two options. 1. A ten-year loan with an annual interest rate of $10\%$ compounded quarterly, with the condition that at the end of 5 years, Alex must make a payment equal to half of what he owes. The other half continues to accrue interest, and at the end of the ten years, Alex will pay off the remaining balance. 2. A ten-year loan with a simple annual interest rate of $12\%$, with just one lump-sum payment at the end of the ten years. Find the positive difference between the total amounts Alex has to pay back under the two schemes. Round your answer to the nearest dollar.

A random sample of 25 men and a separate random sample of 25 women are selected to answer questions about attitudes toward abortion. The answers were categorized as "pro-life" or "pro-choice." Which of the following is the proper null hypothesis for this situation?
options: The proportion of "pro-life" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-life" women is related to the proportion of "pro-choice" men.
The proportion of "pro-choice" women is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" men.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is unrelated to the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is greater than the proportion of "pro-choice" women.
The proportion of "pro-life" men is greater than the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The variables "gender" and "attitude toward abortion" are related.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The proportion of "pro-life" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.

Which of the following statements about protein structure is correct?
options: Cross-links formed by reduction of -SH groups of cysteine to form disulphide bridges render the amino acid unavailable to digestion.
Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of aspartate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.
All proteins have the same secondary structure
The arrangement of amino acids in a protein is its secondary structure
The sequence of amino acids in a protein is its quaternary structure
All proteins have the same tertiary structure
Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of glutamate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.
All proteins have the same primary structure
Cross-links formed by oxidation of -SH groups of cysteine to form disulphide bridges render the amino acid unavailable to digestion.
The sequence of amino acids in a protein is its tertiary structure
attempted_job.correct_answer: Cross-links between the ϵ-amino group of lysine and the carboxyl group of glutamate render the amino acid(s) unavailable for digestion.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
"The greatest contribution that Africa can make to the peace of the world is to avoid all the dangers inherent in disunity, by creating a political union which will also by its success, stand as an example to a divided world. A Union of African states will project more effectively the African personality. It will command respect from a world that has regard only for size and influence. The scant attention paid to African opposition to the French atomic tests in the Sahara, and the ignominious spectacle of the U.N. in the Congo quibbling about constitutional niceties while the Republic was tottering into anarchy, are evidence of the callous disregard of African Independence by the Great Powers."
Kwame Nkrumah, Ghanian leader, 1961
Based on the speech, the French saw Africa as which of the following?
options: A continent that needs French intervention to maintain peace
Important due to the Sahara's natural resources
An example of successful political union
A resource for enhancing French power in the Cold War
An obstacle to French atomic tests in the Sahara
A threat to French power and influence
A continent too divided to be of significance
A collection of republics tottering into anarchy
attempted_job.correct_answer: A resource for enhancing French power in the Cold War

This question refers to the following information.
Oh, shame on you, poor Winter King!
What's this that you have done?
Is 't not a very naughty thing
To snatch the kaiser's crown?
Now you will have to stay away
Alike from Rhine and Prague,
And more than that-shame and dismay
Your days and nights will plague.
Right well you knew, and all the world,
Right well they know this thing,
That Ferdinand alone can be
Bohemia's lawful king.
So come, dear Fritz, rouse up and go
To Ferdinand, your king,
And beg him graciously to show
Full pardon for your sin.
"Poor Winter King," seventeenth-century song
Based on the song, the Winter King did which of the following?
options: Claimed the lands of Bohemia for the Catholic Church
Allied with King Ferdinand against other kingdoms
Stole the crown from the kaiser
Was granted full pardon for his actions by King Ferdinand
Was a symbolic figure without real power
Was a popular folk hero celebrated in songs and stories
Successfully defended his land from the Holy Roman Empire
Gave over his crown to King Ferdinand

A previously healthy 15-year-old boy is brought to the emergency department in August 1 hour after the onset of headache, dizziness, nausea, and one episode of vomiting. His symptoms began during the first hour of full-contact football practice in full uniform. He reported feeling weak and faint but did not lose consciousness. He vomited once after drinking water. On arrival, he is diaphoretic. He is not oriented to person, place, or time. His temperature is 39.5°C (103.1°F), pulse is 120/min, respirations are 40/min, and blood pressure is 90/65 mm Hg. Examination, including neurologic examination, shows no other abnormalities. Which of the following is the most appropriate next step in management?
options: Administer oxygen via a nasal cannula
Immerse the patient in an ice water bath
Apply cold compresses to the forehead
Obtain a CT scan of the head
attempted_job.correct_answer: Immerse the patient in an ice water bath

Shortly after breaking up with the defendant, a woman began dating the victim. The defendant, who still loved the woman, hated the victim. During a holiday weekend, the woman and the victim arranged to go camping in a federal park. The defendant and his friend decided to beat up the victim while he and the woman were on their camping trip. They went to the campsite where the woman and the victim were staying, but they couldn't find the couple, who were hiking in the woods. Subsequently, the defendant was arrested and charged with conspiracy to commit an assault in a federal park. At trial, the defendant testified that he didn't know he was in a federal park. Moreover, he stated that if he had known, he would never have agreed to the crime. If the jury believes the defendant, he should be found
options: guilty, because the location of the crime does not affect the intent to commit the crime.
guilty, because agreeing to commit a crime is enough to be charged with conspiracy.
not guilty, because he did not agree to commit a crime in a federal park.
guilty, because federal conspiracy laws require only an intent to commit a prohibited act, but do not require a knowledge of the surrounding circumstances.
not guilty, because he did not actually find the victim to carry out the assault.
not guilty, because he didn't have the specific intent to commit the crime of assault in a federal park.
not guilty, because he lacked the knowledge that his actions were illegal.
not guilty, because he did not actually commit the assault.
guilty, because federal conspiracy laws do not require the mental retainment ofjurisdictional requirements.
guilty, because his testimony shows that he had an intent to harm the victim.
attempted_job.correct_answer: guilty, because federal conspiracy laws do not require the mental retainment ofjurisdictional requirements.

A grantor executed a deed by which he conveyed his apartment building for a consideration of one dollar, receipt of which was acknowledged, "to my son for life, then to my uncle for life, but if my uncle moves to another state, to my brother for the life of my uncle, then to the heirs of my uncle if my uncle does not move to another state, and to the heirs of my brother if my uncle does move to another state. " This deed was promptly recorded. During the son's lifetime, the brother's interest may best be described as a (an)
options: vested executory interest pur autre vie.
contingent executory interest pur autre vie.
shifting executory interest pur autre vie.
attempted_job.correct_answer: shifting executory interest pur autre vie.

attempted_job.description: A 2-liter Dumas bulb contains n moles of nitrogen at 0.5atm pressure and at T°K. On addition of 0.01 moles of oxygen, it is necessary to cool the bulb to a temperature of 10°C in order to maintain the same pressure. Calculate n and T.
A market demand curve is defined as a horizontal summation of all the individual demand curves. Why is this summation taken horizontally rather than vertically?
options: A vertical summation fits the definition of a market demand curve.
The horizontal summation reflects the prices at which consumers are willing to purchase a given quantity.
A vertical summation would represent the sum of the prices for each quantity, which is not meaningful.
The proper horizontal point is a summation of all the individual quantities demanded.
The market demand curve is a schedule of prices.
The horizontal summation allows for the calculation of the total quantity demanded at various price levels.
A vertical summation would show the total revenue, not the quantity demanded.
A vertical summation would aggregate individual demands in terms of price, not quantity.
The vertical summation would reflect the cumulative willingness to pay, rather than the total quantity demanded.
The vertical point is a summation of all individual quantities demanded.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The proper horizontal point is a summation of all the individual quantities demanded.

options: expression of the genes only when glucose is present
lack of expression or reduced expression of the lac operon genes under all circumstances
no change in the expression of the lac operon genes
expression of the genes only when lactose is absent
constitutive expression of the lac operon genes
expression of the genes only when lactose is present
increased expression of the lac operon genes under all circumstances
expression of the genes only when glucose is absent
constitutive repression of the lac operon genes
reduced expression of the lac operon genes only when lactose is present
attempted_job.correct_answer: lack of expression or reduced expression of the lac operon genes under all circumstances

attempted_job.description: Distinguish between the terms "in situ,\textquotedblright "in vitro," and "invivo."
options: "In situ" refers to reactions in a laboratory setting. "In vitro" refers to reactions in the body of a living organism. "In vivo" refers to reactions observed in a natural setting but not within a living organism.

"In situ" refers to reactions that are artificially induced. "In vitro" refers to reactions that occur naturally within a living organism. "In vivo" refers to reactions studied in a laboratory environment.
"In situ" refers to reactions in a test tube. "In vitro" refers to reactions in the living organism. "In vivo" refers to reactions in their original position.

"In situ" refers to reactions that occur in an artificial organism. "In vitro" refers to reactions that take place in a natural environment. "In vivo" refers to reactions that are simulated using computer models.

"In situ" refers to reactions in a living organism that has been modified. "In vitro" refers to reactions in their natural position. "In vivo" refers to reactions in a test tube or artificial setting.
"In situ" refers to reactions in a controlled laboratory environment. "In vitro" refers to reactions in a natural setting. "In vivo" refers to reactions in a computational simulation.

"In situ" is a reaction that occurs in its natural or original position. "In vitro" is a reaction that occurs in a laboratory such as a test tube. "In vivo" is a reaction that occurs in the living organism.
"In situ" refers to reactions in the living organism. "In vitro" refers to reactions in a test tube. "In vivo" refers to reactions in their original position.
"In situ" refers to reactions occurring in the living organism. "In vitro" refers to reactions in their original position. "In vivo" refers to reactions in a test tube.
"In situ" refers to reactions that occur in a virtual environment. "In vitro" is a reaction that occurs in a living organism. "In vivo" is a reaction that occurs outside of a biological context.
attempted_job.correct_answer: "In situ" is a reaction that occurs in its natural or original position. "In vitro" is a reaction that occurs in a laboratory such as a test tube. "In vivo" is a reaction that occurs in the living organism.

attempted_job.description: When an adult mouse or bird is castrated, its sexual behavior virtually disappears. Compare the effects of castration of highly developed primates with the less developed animals.
options: In higher primates such as man, castration does not affect the ability to perform the sexual act, provided the male is sexually mature and experienced.
In primates, castration after sexual maturity reduces aggression but has no effect on sexual behavior.
Primates show a temporary decline in sexual behavior post-castration, but it fully recovers within weeks.
Castration in primates results in complete loss of sexual behavior
Castration in primates has no effect on their sexual behavior at any stage of development.
Castration of primates leads to an increase in sexual behavior due to hormonal imbalances.
Castration affects the ability to perform sexual acts in all animals
Primates can't learn sexual behavior through experience
Castration of primates causes a permanent shift to non-sexual social behavior patterns.
In higher primates, castration before puberty prevents the development of sexual behavior entirely.
attempted_job.correct_answer: In higher primates such as man, castration does not affect the ability to perform the sexual act, provided the male is sexually mature and experienced.

attempted_job.description: Excess disability refers to symptoms that are
options: Not due to the disease but due instead to malignant social psychology
Those that are experienced only by individuals over a certain age
In excess of the average for that particular disorder
Symptoms that occur as a side effect of treatment for the disease
Symptoms not related to any disease but are due to a mental disorder
Symptoms that are exacerbated by comorbid conditions
Those that appear only after the disease has been cured
Those that only appear in chronic diseases
Those that can be successfully treated by medicine if they are caught early enough
Found only in the last stage of Alzheimer's
attempted_job.correct_answer: Not due to the disease but due instead to malignant social psychology

(a) If the apex angle of the flint-glass prism, A', is 10.0°, find the apex angle of the crown-glass prism, A. (b) Find the net dispersion of the direct-vision prism. (The indices of refraction of the crown-glass and flint-glass prisms are 1.508 and 1.620, respectively.)

Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 10.8 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.111 degrees
Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 17.3 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.031 degrees

Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 8.2 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.121 degrees

Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 11.2 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.091 degrees

Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 13.2 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.071 degrees
Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 16.5 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.041 degrees
attempted_job.correct_answer: Apex angle of the crown-glass prism is 12.2 degrees and net dispersion of the direct-vision prism is 0.081 degrees

An owner entered into a written contract with a landscaper whereby the landscaper promised to landscape the exterior of the owner's house for the sum of $5,000. According to their agreement, the owner was to pay the money to the landscaper's son. The landscaper intended to have the $5,000 given to his son as a graduation present. After the landscaper completed the job, the landscaper requested that the owner pay the $5,000 to him instead of to his son. The owner paid the landscaper. The next day, the son learned of the contract between the landscaper and the owner, as well as the payment to his father. In an action by the son against the owner for $5,000, the plaintiff will most likely
options: prevail, because the written contract between the owner and the landscaper operated as a valid assignment to the son.
prevail, because the son was the intended beneficiary under the terms of the written contract between the owner and the landscaper.
not prevail, because the son did not give any consideration.
prevail, because the owner had a duty to honor the original agreement.
not prevail, because the landscaper, as the original contractor, had the right to alter the terms of payment.
not prevail, because the son was not a party to the original contract.
not prevail, because the son was not directly involved in the contractual agreement.
prevail, because the owner breached the contract by not paying the son.
prevail, because the son was a third party beneficiary of the contract.
not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.
attempted_job.correct_answer: not prevail, because the owner and the landscaper effectively modified their agreement, thereby depriving the son of any rights he may have had.

A grantor executed a deed by which he conveyed his apartment building for a consideration of one dollar, receipt of which was acknowledged, "to my son for life, then to my uncle for life, but if my uncle moves to another state, to my brother for the life of my uncle, then to the heirs of my uncle if my uncle does not move to another state, and to the heirs of my brother if my uncle does move to another state. " This deed was promptly recorded. During the son's lifetime, the brother's interest may best be described as a (an)
options: vested executory interest pur autre vie.
contingent executory interest pur autre vie.
shifting executory interest pur autre vie.
attempted_job.correct_answer: shifting executory interest pur autre vie.

attempted_job.description: Which statement best explains the purpose of Hart's distinction between 'being obliged' and 'having an obligation'?
options: It refutes the natural lawyer' view of the role of morality in law.
It illuminates the concept of a rule.
It differentiates between formal and substantive justice.
It highlights the importance of individual rights in law.
It clarifies the relationship between law and society.
It explains the nature of power-conferring rules.
It distinguishes between legal and ethical obligations.
It provides a guideline for the application of laws.
It explains the role of judicial discretion in the law.
attempted_job.correct_answer: It illuminates the concept of a rule.

A random sample of 25 men and a separate random sample of 25 women are selected to answer questions about attitudes toward abortion. The answers were categorized as "pro-life" or "pro-choice." Which of the following is the proper null hypothesis for this situation?
options: The proportion of "pro-life" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-life" women is related to the proportion of "pro-choice" men.
The proportion of "pro-choice" women is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" men.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is unrelated to the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is greater than the proportion of "pro-choice" women.
The proportion of "pro-life" men is greater than the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The proportion of "pro-choice" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.
The variables "gender" and "attitude toward abortion" are related.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The proportion of "pro-life" men is the same as the proportion of "pro-life" women.

Estimate the PE ratio for a firm that has the following characteristics:
Length of high growth = five years
Growth rate in first five years = 25%
Payout ratio in first five years = 20%
Growth rate after five years = 8%
Payout ratio after five years = 50%
Beta = 1.0 
Risk-free rate = T-bond rate = 6%
Cost of equity = 6% + 1(5.5%) = 11.5%
Risk premium = 5.5%
What is the estimated PE ratio for this firm?

A man and a woman agreed that the woman would rob a bank and that the man would steal a car beforehand for the woman to use as a getaway vehicle. The man stole a car and parked it two blocks from the bank. He left the car key under the floor mat with a note saying that he wanted nothing more to do with the scheme. The next day, the woman robbed the bank and ran to the spot where the man had said he would leave the stolen car. She then escaped in the car and disappeared. She never shared any of the money with the man. In a jurisdiction that has adopted the bilateral requirement for conspiracy, can the man properly be convicted of conspiring with the woman to rob the bank?
options: Yes, because the man was a part of the initial planning of the robbery.
Yes, because the man committed a crime (stealing a car) in furtherance of the conspiracy.
Yes, because the robbery was successful due in part to the man's actions.
Yes, because the man provided the getaway vehicle.
Yes, because there was an agreement to rob the bank and an overt act in furtherance of the agreement.
No, because the man expressed his intention to withdraw from the conspiracy.
No, because the man received no benefit from the robbery.
No, because the woman did not share the money with the man.
No, because the man did not participate in the actual robbery.
attempted_job.correct_answer: Yes, because there was an agreement to rob the bank and an overt act in furtherance of the agreement.

attempted_job.description:
This question refers to the following information.
Oh, shame on you, poor Winter King!
What's this that you have done?
Is 't not a very naughty thing
To snatch the kaiser's crown?
Now you will have to stay away
Alike from Rhine and Prague,
And more than that-shame and dismay
Your days and nights will plague.
Right well you knew, and all the world,
Right well they know this thing,
That Ferdinand alone can be
Bohemia's lawful king.
So come, dear Fritz, rouse up and go
To Ferdinand, your king,
And beg him graciously to show
Full pardon for your sin.
"Poor Winter King," seventeenth-century song
Based on the song, the Winter King did which of the following?
options: Claimed the lands of Bohemia for the Catholic Church
Allied with King Ferdinand against other kingdoms
Stole the crown from the kaiser
Was granted full pardon for his actions by King Ferdinand
Was a symbolic figure without real power
Was a popular folk hero celebrated in songs and stories
Successfully defended his land from the Holy Roman Empire
Gave over his crown to King Ferdinand

attempted_job.description: Let G = (V, E) be a connected, undirected graph, and let a and b be two distinct vertices in V. Let P_1 be the problem of finding a shortest simple path between a and b, and let P_2 be the problem of finding a longest simple path between a and b. Which of the following statements about P_1 and P_2 is true?
options: Both P_1 and P_2 are known to not be solvable in polynomial time.
Both P_1 and P_2 can be solved in polynomial time.
P_1 is not known to be solvable in polynomial time but P_2 can be solved in polynomial time.
P_2 can be solved in polynomial time and P_1 is known to not be solvable in polynomial time.
P_2 can be solved in polynomial time but P_1 is not known to be solvable in polynomial time.
It is known that either P_1 or P_2 can be solved in polynomial time.
P_1 can be solved in polynomial time but P_2 is not known to be solvable in polynomial time.
It is not known whether either P_1 or P_2 can be solved in polynomial time.
P_1 is known to not be solvable in polynomial time but P_2 can be solved in polynomial time.
P_1 can be solved in polynomial time and P_2 is known to not be solvable in polynomial time.
attempted_job.correct_answer: P_1 can be solved in polynomial time but P_2 is not known to be solvable in polynomial time.

This question refers to the following information.
"The law of love, peace and liberty in the states extending to Jews, Turks and Egyptians, as they are considered sonnes of Adam, which is the glory of the outward state of Holland, soe love, peace and liberty, extending to all in Christ Jesus, condemns hatred, war and bondage. And because our Saviour sayeth it is impossible but that offences will come, but woe unto him by whom they cometh, our desire is not to offend one of his little ones, in whatsoever form, name or title hee appears in, whether Presbyterian, Independent, Baptist or Quaker, but shall be glad to see anything of God in any of them, desiring to doe unto all men as we desire all men should doe unto us, which is the true law both of Church and State; for our Saviour sayeth this is the law and the prophets.
"Therefore if any of these said persons come in love unto us, we cannot in conscience lay violent hands upon them, but give them free egresse and regresse unto our Town, and houses, as God shall persuade our consciences, for we are bounde by the law of God and man to doe good unto all men and evil to noe man. And this is according to the patent and charter of our Towne, given unto us in the name of the States General, which we are not willing to infringe, and violate, but shall houlde to our patent and shall remaine, your humble subjects, the inhabitants of Vlishing (Flushing, part of the colony of New Netherlands)."
—The Flushing Remonstrance, 1657
Which of the following was most significant in enshrining into the U.S. legal structure the ideas contained in the Flushing Remonstrance?
options: The enumeration of congressional powers in the Constitution.
The "Right to Bear Arms" clause of the Second Amendment.
The "Due Process Clause" of the Fifth Amendment.
The "Right to a Speedy and Public Trial" clause of the Sixth Amendment.
The "Cruel and Unusual Punishment" clause of the Eighth Amendment.
The "Establishment Clause" of the First Amendment.
The "Free Exercise Clause" of the First Amendment.
The preamble of the Declaration of Independence.
The "Double Jeopardy" clause of the Fifth Amendment.
attempted_job.correct_answer: The "Free Exercise Clause" of the First Amendment.
